title: angelica anomala var. purpuraefolia
intro: Angelica anomala var. purpuraefolia, also known as Purple-leaf Angelica, is a variety of the Angelica anomala plant, a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat a range of health conditions. The health benefits of Purple-leaf Angelica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional Asian medicine. Herbal preparations of Purple-leaf Angelica may be taken as teas, infusions, or dried extracts, which can be used to make a variety of medicinal products, such as tonics and capsules.
